name: Pakistan
summary: Day 63 Thursday. PAKISTAN MEDIATOR ROLE STABILIZES — QATAR PUBLICLY DECLINES TO LEAD; SUPPORTS PAKISTAN AS PRIMARY CHANNEL ('WE DON'T NEED TO EXPAND THE CIRCLE' — al-ANSARI). The Apr 28 trajectory of 'Pakistan mediator role degrading + Qatar emerging' has REVERSED into 'Pakistan stabilizes as primary + Qatar in supporting/parallel-channel role.' Qatari Foreign Ministry Spokesman Majed al-Ansari publicly stated April 29 that Qatar SUPPORTS Pakistan's mediation and that Doha is coordinating with Islamabad to the point that 'we do not need to expand the circle of negotiations' (Voice of Emirates, Tribune India, ANI News). Yesterday's brief watching trigger 'Qatar formally named primary or co-mediator within 14 days' is structurally answered NO. Architecture clarified as MULTI-PARTY: Pakistan + Qatar + Egypt + Turkey + Oman with Pakistan still primary. Iran's FM Araghchi told Pakistani, Egyptian, Turkish AND Qatari mediators over the weekend that 'there's no consensus inside the Iranian leadership about how to address the U.S. demands' — Iran is communicating internal deadlock through ALL four channels simultaneously, which itself signals IRGC consolidation has not produced operational unity (the multi-channel paralysis IS the signal). Reuters Apr 28 wire-tier analysis 'Iran's Guards Seize Wartime Power' was confirmed by 1 PAKISTANI source + 2 Iranian sources — Pakistani-source role in confirming Vahidi-as-central-interlocutor framework CONFIRMS Pakistan's continued operational involvement. [TRUNCATED — needs re-synthesis]
